segments.caption: The video begins with a black screen displaying the word 'HELLO' in white, followed by a stylized logo and Chinese text 'зҘһеҘҮе°ҸзҢӘ' (Magic Pig) beneath it. The scene transitions to a beige background with a title in Chinese: '2025 ж–°й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·зҘһз»Ҹйўҳзӣ®иөҸжһҗ' (2025 New Gaokao Paper I Nerve-Wracking Problem Appreciation), accompanied by a laughing emoji and a math problem involving the function f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x, asking for its maximum value on the interval [0, ПҖ/4]. The video then shifts to a black screen with white Chinese text 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§ҜдёҺз§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®' (Sum-to-Product and Product-to-Sum Identities). This is followed by a detailed slide showing trigonometric identities divided into two columns: the left column (highlighted with an orange bar) lists product-to-sum identities, and the right column (highlighted with a blue bar) lists sum-to-product identities. The video then transitions to another slide titled 'жҲ‘д»¬еӯҰиҝҮзҡ„дёӨи§’е’Ңе·®' (The Sum and Difference of Two Angles WeвҖҷve Learned), displaying four standard trigonometric identities for cos(ОұВұОІ) and sin(ОұВұОІ). The identities are sequentially highlighted with red circular markers, and the video demonstrates deriving the identity cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)] by adding cos(Оұ+ОІ) and cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ), resulting in 2cosОұcosОІ, which is then rewritten as ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]. The derivation is annotated with handwritten-style text and arrows, emphasizing the transformation process.

discovered_new_entities.Derivation Annotation: Handwritten-style text and arrows appearing on the Two-Angle Identities slide, showing the derivation: 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ' and then 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', with a red arrow linking the two expressions.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Trig Identities Slide, displaying a beige background with a yellow title 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§ҜдёҺз§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®' (Sum-to-Product and Product-to-Sum Identities). Two columns of formulas are shown: the left column (marked with an orange bar) lists product-to-sum identities, and the right column (marked with a blue bar) lists sum-to-product identities. A red circular marker sequentially highlights each formula in the left column, starting with 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', then moving to 'sinОұsinОІ = вҲ’ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) вҲ’ cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', followed by 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', and finally 'cosОұsinОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) вҲ’ sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]'. The video then transitions to the Two-Angle Identities Slide, which features a yellow title 'жҲ‘д»¬еӯҰиҝҮзҡ„дёӨи§’е’Ңе·®' (The Two-Angle Sum and Difference Identities We've Learned) with a laughing emoji. The four standard identities for cos(ОұВұОІ) and sin(ОұВұОІ) are displayed, and red circular markers highlight each one as they are discussed. Handwritten-style annotations appear below, showing the derivation: 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ' and 'sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2sinОұcosОІ', with red arrows linking these to the derived product-to-sum formulas: 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]' and 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]'. The annotations are written in blue and red ink, with red circles emphasizing key terms in the derivation process.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Two-Angle Identities Slide, displaying four standard trigonometric identities for cos(ОұВұОІ) and sin(ОұВұОІ) under the yellow title 'жҲ‘д»¬еӯҰиҝҮзҡ„дёӨи§’е’Ңе·®' with a laughing emoji. Red circular markers appear sequentially to highlight each identity as the derivation process is explained. Handwritten-style annotations in blue and red ink are added to show the derivation of product-to-sum formulas: 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ' and 'sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2sinОұcosОІ'. Red arrows link these to the derived formulas 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]' and 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', with red circles emphasizing key terms. The derivation continues with substitutions: 'A = Оұ+ОІ' and 'B = ОұвҲ’ОІ', leading to the general forms 'cosA + cosB = 2cos((A+B)/2)cos((AвҲ’B)/2)' and 'sinA + sinB = 2sin((A+B)/2)cos((AвҲ’B)/2)'. The scene then transitions to the Trig Identities Slide, titled 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§ҜдёҺз§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®', which organizes the formulas into two columns: the left (orange bar) for product-to-sum and the right (blue bar) for sum-to-product. Red circular markers sequentially highlight each formula in the left column, while the right column displays the derived sum-to-product identities. The video concludes with the derivation of 'sinA - sinB = 2cos((A+B)/2)sin((AвҲ’B)/2)', with a red circle emphasizing the 'sinA - sinB' expression.

segments.caption: The video segment focuses on the Trig Identities Slide, which displays a beige background with a yellow title 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§ҜдёҺз§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®' (Sum-to-Product and Product-to-Sum Identities). The slide is divided into two columns: the left column, marked with an orange bar, lists product-to-sum formulas, and the right column, marked with a blue bar, lists sum-to-product formulas. Red circular markers sequentially highlight each formula in the left column, starting with 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', which is circled in red. The video then transitions to the right column, where red circular markers highlight the sum-to-product formulas, beginning with 'sinA + sinB = 2sin[(A+B)/2]cos[(AвҲ’B)/2]'. As the video progresses, handwritten-style annotations appear above the formulas, showing a mnemonic: 'SВ·C + CВ·S' for 'sinA + sinB' and 'SВ·C вҲ’ CВ·S' for 'sinA вҲ’ sinB'. The annotation 'sinA вҲ’ sinB = 2cos[(A+B)/2]sin[(AвҲ’B)/2]' is then written out in red ink, with the final formula being underlined in red. The visual flow emphasizes the derivation and memorization of these identities through color-coded highlighting and mnemonic cues.

segments.caption: The video segment focuses on the Trig Identities Slide, which displays a beige background with a yellow title 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§ҜдёҺз§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®' (Sum-to-Product and Product-to-Sum Identities). The slide is divided into two columns: the left column (marked with an orange vertical bar) lists product-to-sum formulas, and the right column (marked with a blue vertical bar) lists sum-to-product formulas. The video begins with a red circular marker highlighting the third formula in the left column, 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', which is then circled in red. The presenter then writes 'cosA - cosB =' in red ink below the slide, followed by the mnemonic 'CВ·CвҲ’SВ·S' above it, with arrows pointing to '(О”+О”)' and '(О”вҲ’О”)' to indicate the structure of the resulting formula. The presenter then writes the full formula 'cosA - cosB = -2sin[(A+B)/2]sin[(AвҲ’B)/2]' in red ink, aligning it with the corresponding formula in the right column. The red circular markers then sequentially highlight each formula in the left column (starting from the top) and then move to the right column, emphasizing the connection between the product-to-sum and sum-to-product identities. The handwritten annotations above the formulas, such as 'SВ·C+CВ·S' and 'SВ·CвҲ’CВ·S', serve as memory aids for the signs in the formulas. The overall visual flow is instructional, with deliberate highlighting and writing to guide the viewer through the derivation and memorization of the identities.

The first problem states: 'е·ІзҹҘ sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4, cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3, еҲҷ tan(Оұ+ОІ) зҡ„еҖјдёә ________.' (Given sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4, cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3, find the value of tan(Оұ+ОІ).), marked with a red dot and labeled '2025 е№ҝдёңжңҲиҖғ' (2025 Guangdong Monthly Exam). (Let the function f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x, find the maximum value of f(x) on [0, ПҖ/4].), marked with a blue dot and labeled '2025 й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·' (2025 National Exam Paper I). Red circular markers sequentially highlight 'sinОұ + sinОІ', 'cosОұ + cosОІ', and 'tan(Оұ+ОІ)' in the first problem, while handwritten-style annotations 'SВ·C + CВ·S' and 'SВ·C - CВ·S' appear above the problem to guide the solution process.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with the Math Problem Slide, displaying two trigonometric problems on a beige background. The top problem, marked '2025 е№ҝдёңжңҲиҖғ', asks for tan(Оұ+ОІ) given sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4 and cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3. The bottom problem, marked '2025 й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·', asks for the maximum of f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x on [0, ПҖ/4]. Red circular markers appear sequentially to highlight parts of the first problem. Handwritten-style annotations 'SВ·C + CВ·S' and 'SВ·C - CВ·S' appear above the first problem, indicating the sum-to-product identities. The video then shows the derivation of the sum-to-product formulas: '2sin[(Оұ+ОІ)/2]cos[(ОұвҲ’ОІ)/2] = 1/4' and '2cos[(Оұ+ОІ)/2]cos[(ОұвҲ’ОІ)/2] = 1/3' are written below the given equations. A red horizontal line is drawn between these two derived equations, and the result 'tan[(Оұ+ОІ)/2] = 3/4' is written to the right, indicating the solution to the first problem. The red markers continue to guide the viewer through the steps, emphasizing the division of the two equations to find the tangent value.

active_entities.Math Problem Slide: A beige background with a yellow title 'зүӣеҲҖе°ҸиҜ•' and two math problems: the first involves finding tan(Оұ+ОІ) given sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4 and cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3, marked as '2025 е№ҝдёңжңҲиҖғ'; the second involves finding the maximum of f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x on [0, ПҖ/4], marked as '2025 й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·'. Red circular markers highlight parts of the first problem, and handwritten-style annotations 'SВ·C + CВ·S' and 'SВ·C - CВ·S' appear above it. The derivation of sum-to-product formulas is shown below the given equations, with a red line drawn between them and the result 'tan[(Оұ+ОІ)/2] = 3/4' written to the right.

segments.caption: The video segment continues on the Math Problem Slide, which displays two trigonometric problems on a beige background with a yellow title 'зүӣеҲҖе°ҸиҜ•'. The first problem, marked '2025 е№ҝдёңжңҲиҖғ', involves finding tan(Оұ+ОІ) given sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4 and cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3. The derivation using sum-to-product formulas is shown below, with red lines and annotations. The solution process is animated: the expression for tan(Оұ+ОІ) is built step-by-step, starting with '2Г—3/4' over '1вҲ’(3/4)ВІ', simplifying to '3/2 Г· 7/16', and finally yielding '24/7'. The second problem, marked '2025 й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·', asks for the maximum of f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x on [0, ПҖ/4]. The derivative f'(x) is computed in real-time: first as '-5sinx + 5sin5x', then factored into '5(sin5x - sinx)'. Red circular markers appear sequentially to highlight key parts of the second problem, including the function, interval, and the evolving derivative expression. The handwritten-style annotations 'SВ·C + CВ·S' and 'SВ·C - CВ·S' remain above the first problem, and the red line connecting the sum-to-product formulas to the result 'tan[(Оұ+ОІ)/2] = 3/4' is still visible.

active_entities.Math Problem Slide: A beige background with a yellow title 'зүӣеҲҖе°ҸиҜ•' and two math problems: the first involves finding tan(Оұ+ОІ) given sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4 and cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3, marked as '2025 е№ҝдёңжңҲиҖғ'; the second involves finding the maximum of f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x on [0, ПҖ/4], marked as '2025 й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·'. Red circular markers highlight parts of the first problem, and handwritten-style annotations 'SВ·C + CВ·S' and 'SВ·C - CВ·S' appear above it. The derivation of sum-to-product formulas is shown below the given equations, with a red line drawn between them and the result 'tan[(Оұ+ОІ)/2] = 3/4' written to the right. The solution for the first problem is animated, showing the step-by-step calculation of tan(Оұ+ОІ) = 24/7. The derivative f'(x) = -5sinx + 5sin5x = 5(sin5x - sinx) is written out for the second problem, with red markers appearing on the function, interval, and derivative expression.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Math Problem Slide, displaying two trigonometric problems on a beige background under the yellow title 'зүӣеҲҖе°ҸиҜ•'. The first problem involves finding tan(Оұ+ОІ) given sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4 and cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3, marked as '2025 е№ҝдёңжңҲиҖғ', with red circular markers highlighting parts of the problem and handwritten annotations 'SВ·C + CВ·S' and 'SВ·C - CВ·S' above. The derivation of sum-to-product formulas is shown below, with a red line connecting the given equations to the result 'tan[(Оұ+ОІ)/2] = 3/4', and the final calculation 'tan(Оұ+ОІ) = 24/7' is animated. The second problem, marked '2025 й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·', asks for the maximum of f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x on [0, ПҖ/4], with its derivative f'(x) = -5sinx + 5sin5x = 5(sin5x - sinx) written out, and red markers appearing on the function, interval, and derivative. The scene transitions to the Trig Identities Slide, titled 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§ҜдёҺз§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®', showing two columns of identities: product-to-sum (left, orange bar) and sum-to-product (right, blue bar). Red circular markers sequentially highlight each formula in the left column, followed by the right column, with handwritten annotations like 'CВ·CвҲ’SВ·S' and 'cosA - cosB = -2sin[(A+B)/2]sin[(AвҲ’B)/2]' appearing in red ink. The video then returns to the Math Problem Slide, where the focus shifts to the second problem. The derivative f'(x) = 5(sin5x - sinx) is further simplified using the sum-to-product identity, with annotations 'SВ·C + CВ·S' and 'SВ·C - CВ·S' appearing above the expression. The simplification proceeds step-by-step: '5(sin5x - sinx) = 10 cos 3x В· sin 2x', with red markers appearing on 'sin5x', 'sinx', 'cos 3x', and 'sin 2x' as each term is transformed, culminating in the fully simplified derivative expression.

active_entities.Math Problem Slide: A beige background with a yellow title 'зүӣеҲҖе°ҸиҜ•' and two math problems: the first involves finding tan(Оұ+ОІ) given sinОұ + sinОІ = 1/4 and cosОұ + cosОІ = 1/3, marked as '2025 е№ҝдёңжңҲиҖғ'; the second involves finding the maximum of f(x) = 5cosx - cos5x on [0, ПҖ/4], marked as '2025 й«ҳиҖғдёҖеҚ·'. segments.caption: The video segment begins with a beige background and a yellow title 'жҝҖжғ…еӨ§жҢ‘жҲҳ' (Passionate Challenge) at the top, accompanied by a laughing emoji. The main content is a trigonometric function problem: f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, with four multiple-choice options (A to D) listing possible minimum or maximum values. The problem is marked as from '2018 жё…еҚҺеӨ§еӯҰиҮӘдё»жӢӣз”ҹ' (2018 Tsinghua University Independent Enrollment) in the bottom right. The video then focuses on the numerator of the fraction, with red circular markers appearing sequentially on 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' and 'cos3xВ·cosВіx'. A red curved arrow is drawn from 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' to a handwritten annotation below, which breaks it down as 'sin3xВ·sinxВ·sinВІx'. Simultaneously, on the right side of the screen, handwritten trigonometric identities appear: 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) = CВ·C - SВ·S', 'cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = CВ·C + SВ·S', and then 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) - cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = -2В·sinОұВ·sinОІ', with a red curved arrow pointing to the final result. The red markers and annotations are used to guide the viewer through the process of simplifying the expression using trigonometric identities, specifically targeting the product-to-sum formulas derived from the sum and difference identities.

active_entities.Trig Identities Slide: A beige background with a yellow title 'жҝҖжғ…еӨ§жҢ‘жҲҳ' and a trigonometric function problem. The problem is f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, with four multiple-choice options. Red circular markers highlight parts of the numerator, and handwritten annotations appear below and to the right, showing the breakdown of 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' into 'sin3xВ·sinxВ·sinВІx' and deriving the identity 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) - cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = -2В·sinОұВ·sinОІ' from the standard sum and difference formulas for cosine, with red arrows and circles emphasizing the derivation steps.

segments.caption: The video segment continues on the 'Trig Identities Slide', which displays a complex trigonometric function problem titled 'жҝҖжғ…еӨ§жҢ‘жҲҳ' (Passionate Big Challenge) from the 2018 Tsinghua University Independent Enrollment exam. The function is f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, with four multiple-choice options for its minimum or maximum value. The video focuses on breaking down the numerator. A red circle highlights 'sin3xВ·sinВіx', and a red arrow points to a handwritten derivation below: 'sin3xВ·sinxВ·sinВІx' is rewritten as '-ВҪ[cos4x - cos2x]В·sinВІx', using the identity derived from the cosine sum and difference formulas shown on the right. Then, a blue circle highlights 'cos3xВ·cosВіx', and a blue arrow points to its breakdown: 'cos3xВ·cosxВ·cosВІx' is rewritten as 'ВҪ[cos4x + cos2x]В·cosВІx'. The derivation uses the identity 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', which is visually linked to the standard formulas on the right. The video emphasizes the structure of the expression, showing how the two parts of the numerator are being simplified using product-to-sum identities, with red and blue annotations clearly separating the two components. The final expression for the numerator is shown as the sum of these two simplified terms, with the red and blue parts visually aligned for comparison.

active_entities.Trig Identities Slide: A beige background with a yellow title 'жҝҖжғ…еӨ§жҢ‘жҲҳ' and a trigonometric function problem. The problem is f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, with four multiple-choice options. Red and blue circular markers highlight parts of the numerator, and handwritten annotations appear below and to the right, showing the breakdown of 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' into '-ВҪ[cos4x - cos2x]В·sinВІx' and 'cos3xВ·cosВіx' into 'ВҪ[cos4x + cos2x]В·cosВІx', with red and blue arrows linking to the derivations. The right side displays the standard cosine sum and difference formulas, with red circles emphasizing the derivation of 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) - cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = -2В·sinОұВ·sinОІ' and 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ'.
active_entities.Derivation Annotation: Handwritten-style text and arrows appearing on the Trig Identities Slide, showing the derivation: 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ' and 'sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2sinОұcosОІ', with red arrows linking to the derived formulas 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]' and 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', written in blue and red ink with red circles emphasizing key terms. The derivation is now applied to the problem, with the red part showing the simplification of 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' and the blue part showing the simplification of 'cos3xВ·cosВіx'.

segments.caption: The video segment continues the detailed derivation of the trigonometric function f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, presented on the 'жҝҖжғ…еӨ§жҢ‘жҲҳ' slide. The focus is on simplifying the numerator by breaking down the two terms separately. The red-highlighted term 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' is rewritten as 'sin3xВ·sinxВ·sinВІx', which is then expressed as '-ВҪ[cos4x - cos2x]В·sinВІx'. The blue-highlighted term 'cos3xВ·cosВіx' is rewritten as 'cos3xВ·cosxВ·cosВІx', then as 'ВҪ[cos4x + cos2x]В·cosВІx'. The video then substitutes the identities sinВІx = (1 - cos2x)/2 and cosВІx = (1 + cos2x)/2 into the expressions. After substitution, the two terms are combined, and the resulting expression is simplified by distributing and combining like terms. The final simplified form of the numerator is shown as 'Вј(-cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x - cosВІ2x + cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x + cosВІ2x)'. The video then cancels out the terms '-cosВІ2x' and '+cosВІ2x', and combines '-cos4x + cos4x' to zero, leaving 'Вј(2cos2x + 2cos4xВ·cos2x)'. The segment ends with the expression simplified to 'Вј(2cos2x + 2cos4xВ·cos2x)', which is then further simplified to 'ВҪ(cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x)'. The right side of the screen consistently displays the standard cosine sum and difference formulas, with red circles emphasizing the derivation of 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) - cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = -2В·sinОұВ·sinОІ' and 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ'. The bottom right corner remains marked with '2018 жё…еҚҺеӨ§еӯҰиҮӘдё»жӢӣз”ҹ'.

active_entities.Trig Identities Slide: A beige background with a yellow title 'жҝҖжғ…еӨ§жҢ‘жҲҳ' and a trigonometric function problem. The problem is f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, with four multiple-choice options. Red and blue circular markers highlight parts of the numerator, and handwritten annotations appear below and to the right, showing the breakdown of 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' into '-ВҪ[cos4x - cos2x]В·sinВІx' and 'cos3xВ·cosВіx' into 'ВҪ[cos4x + cos2x]В·cosВІx', with red and blue arrows linking to the derivations. The right side displays the standard cosine sum and difference formulas, with red circles emphasizing the derivation of 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) - cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = -2В·sinОұВ·sinОІ' and 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ'. The derivation continues with substitutions for sinВІx and cosВІx, leading to the combined expression 'Вј(-cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x - cosВІ2x + cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x + cosВІ2x)', which is then simplified to 'ВҪ(cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x).
active_entities.Derivation Annotation: Handwritten-style text and arrows appearing on the Trig Identities Slide, showing the derivation: 'cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2cosОұcosОІ' and 'sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ) = 2sinОұcosОІ', with red arrows linking to the derived formulas 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]' and 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', written in blue and red ink with red circles emphasizing key terms. The derivation is now applied to the problem, with the red part showing the simplification of 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' and the blue part showing the simplification of 'cos3xВ·cosВіx', leading to the combined expression 'Вј(-cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x - cosВІ2x + cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x + cosВІ2x)', which is then simplified to 'ВҪ(cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x).

segments.caption: The video segment begins with the Trig Identities Slide, displaying a complex trigonometric function problem from the 2018 Tsinghua University independent admission exam. The problem is f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, with four multiple-choice options. Red and blue circular markers highlight the two terms in the numerator, and handwritten annotations below show the step-by-step derivation. The red part simplifies 'sin3xВ·sinВіx' into '-ВҪ[cos4x - cos2x]В·sinВІx', while the blue part simplifies 'cos3xВ·cosВіx' into 'ВҪ[cos4x + cos2x]В·cosВІx'. The derivation continues with substitutions for sinВІx and cosВІx using the identity 1 - cos2x/2 and 1 + cos2x/2, respectively. The combined expression is expanded into 'Вј(-cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x - cosВІ2x + cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x + cosВІ2x)', which is then simplified to 'ВҪ(cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x). The video then transitions to a new slide titled 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§ҜдёҺз§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®' (Sum-to-Product and Product-to-Sum Identities), which lists key trigonometric identities on both sides. The left side, labeled 'з§ҜеҢ–е’Ңе·®' (Product-to-Sum), shows formulas like 'cosОұcosОІ = ВҪ[cos(Оұ+ОІ) + cos(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]' and 'sinОұcosОІ = ВҪ[sin(Оұ+ОІ) + sin(ОұвҲ’ОІ)]', with the latter circled in red. The right side, labeled 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§Ҝ' (Sum-to-Product), shows formulas like 'cosA + cosB = 2cos[(A+B)/2]cos[(AвҲ’B)/2]' and 'sinA + sinB = 2sin[(A+B)/2]cos[(AвҲ’B)/2]', with 'sinA + sinB' circled in red. The video concludes with a black screen displaying the text 'е®Ңз»“ж’’иҠұ!' (The End, Confetti!) in bold orange and white font, signaling the end of the segment.

The problem is f(x) = [sin3xВ·sinВіx + cos3xВ·cosВіx]/cosВІ2x + sin2x, with four multiple-choice options. The derivation continues with substitutions for sinВІx and cosВІx, leading to the combined expression 'Вј(-cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x - cosВІ2x + cos4x + cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x + cosВІ2x)', which is then simplified to 'ВҪ(cos2x + cos4xВ·cos2x).

The right side, labeled 'е’Ңе·®еҢ–з§Ҝ', shows formulas like 'cosA + cosB = 2cos[(A+B)/2]cos[(AвҲ’B)/2]' and 'sinA + sinB = 2sin[(A+B)/2]cos[(AвҲ’B)/2]', with 'sinA + sinB' circled in red. segments.caption: The video segment displays a static black screen with bold, centered text in orange and white that reads 'е®Ңз»“ж’’иҠұ!' (The End, Confetti!). This serves as a concluding title card, signaling the end of the video content. There are no animations, transitions, or additional visual elements present. The atmosphere is simple and direct, emphasizing finality and celebration with the use of the exclamation mark and the phrase 'ж’’иҠұ!' (confetti), which is commonly used in Chinese internet culture to mark a joyful conclusion. The screen remains unchanged throughout the entire minute, maintaining a consistent and uncluttered visual focus on the text.
